key_insight: The paper shows that stellarator coil shape and support clamp placement can be optimized together with differentiable FEA to improve structural safety without sacrificing magnetic performance.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, less manual design of fusion reactor coil systems by directly accounting for stress, deformation, and manufacturability in the optimization loop.
method: Integrated coil optimization with support-structure design using coil-fem.
Used differentiable structural mechanics and FEA inside the stellarator coil loop.
Added a stress-based penalty to guide clamp locations and reduce coil-body von Mises stress.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a combined coil-support design with 2.4× lower RMS von Mises stress and similar magnetic field error versus an unoptimized baseline.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate differentiable structural models and may still require careful validation against full high-fidelity engineering analyses and fabrication constraints.
paper_title: Towards joint optimization of stellarator coils and support structures
